flashcards.back: The consistency of data stored in multiple local caches for a shared resource in multiprocessor systems.

flashcards.front: Name the four states in the MESI protocol.
What is the difference between write-invalidate and write-update protocols?
flashcards.back: Write-invalidate invalidates other copies on write, while write-update immediately updates all copies.
When unrelated variables on the same cache line cause unnecessary cache invalidations and performance degradation.

flashcards.front: When would you use directory-based instead of snooping cache coherence?
flashcards.back: In large-scale multiprocessor systems where broadcast-based snooping becomes inefficient due to bus contention.
